channel.title: Killen Time Update
channel.description: Daily briefing on AI, prediction markets, NBA, and entertainment. Hosted by Basil and Brooke. New episodes every 6 hours.
channel.itunes:author: Kyle Killen

item.title: Killen Time — 2026-04-11
item.description: Mythos cybersecurity deep dive: Zvi breaks down the staggering vulnerability findings, Project Glasswing, and what it means for defenders. Plus Anthropic enterprise data from Ramp, OpenAI legal and talent troubles, and Manifold prediction markets on AI economics.

item.title: Killen Time — 2026-04-12
item.description: Self-improving open-source AI from MiniMax, Claude Code dominating HumanX, the Jevons Paradox in AI compute, Nate Silver on synthetic polling dangers, SpaceX two-trillion-dollar IPO and capital competition, NBA playoff preview, Coachella and SNL highlights, and the structural gold thesis from Gavekal.

item.title: Killen Time — 2026-04-13
item.description: AI power concentration and the Piketty thesis from Noah Smith, Altman home attacks, prediction markets hitting record volume with CFTC moves and the California governor race collapse, Gallup 50% AI adoption milestone, NBA playoff race, and entertainment industry updates.

item.title: Killen Time — 2026-04-14
item.description: Anthropic vs OpenAI on the Illinois AI liability shield, Narasimhan joins the Anthropic board ahead of a potential IPO, Treasury wants Mythos access, Zvi on Claude Mythos capabilities, Gouker maps the prediction market startup ecosystem, Polymarket audits its Builders Program, and Robin Hanson asks why we ban sports bets.

item.title: Killen Time — 2026-04-15
item.description: Masters traded half a billion at Kalshi but the real number is way smaller. Iran war splits global energy map in two. LaMelo trips Bam in play-in OT while Amazon goes to technical difficulties. Macro hedge funds using AI to trade the oil shock. Replit CEO says not coding is an advantage. Plus Nate Silver on why engagement metrics are ruining everything.

item.title: Killen Time — 2026-04-16
item.description: Today's Killen Time Update covering the biggest stories in AI, prediction markets, NBA, entertainment, and economics.

item.title: Killen Time — 2026-05-15
item.description: Today's Killen Time Update. After a three-day automation outage, this episode covers the Ben Thompson Inference Shift piece, Scott Alexander on AI sigmoid skeptics, PredMTrader's Trump-Xi bilateral positions, NBA playoff Game Sixes, Cannes, and macro updates from Martin Wolf and Tyler Cowen.

item.title: HERO by Thomas Perry — Audiobook (Part 1 of 5)
item.description: Personal text-to-speech audiobook (Brooke / Kokoro voice) of HERO by Thomas Perry — Captivate review material rendered for Kyle's road trip. Personal use only; will be removed after the drive.

item.title: Fleet Brief — Tiered Failover Proposal (action needed)
item.description: Fleet Optimizer proposal: a self-hosted gateway with per-workload key tiering so the podcast can never drain the trading pool. Money-adjacent — needs Kyle's sign-off. Personal/internal brief, Brooke voice.

item.title: Fleet Brief — Today's Podcast Optimization Harvest
item.description: Fleet Optimizer's ranked harvest of fleet-optimization ideas from today's Killen Time agents/building beat: what's already in place, what I'm acting on, what to greenlight, and the hardware/model decisions to keep warm. Personal/internal brief, Brooke voice.

item.title: Killen Time — Gemini Edition (offload A/B)
item.description: Full episode written entirely by Gemini Flash (free offload, $0), per-segment to reach length. A/B vs the Claude-written shows — same beats, Brooke/Basil voices.

item.title: Killen Time — All-Gemini Edition (zero Claude)
item.description: Whole show on Gemini, no Claude anywhere: fresh ingest (Python+Whisper), build-pitch via Gemini Google-Search grounding, written by Gemini Flash, rendered locally. Free/$0 — fully decoupled from the Claude pool. A/B vs the Claude shows.
